references.text: [ErRa56] Erd\H{o}s, P. and Rado, R., A partition calculus in set theory. Bull. Amer. Math. Soc. (1956), 427-489.

remarks.text: A problem of Erd\H{o}s and Hajnal. The Erd\H{o}s-Rado partition theorem \cite{ErRa56} states that\[(2^{\kappa})^+ \to (\kappa^++1)_\kappa^2\]for every infinite cardinal $\kappa$.

statement.text: Establish whether the following are true assuming the generalised continuum hypothesis:\[\omega_3 \to (\omega_2,\omega_1+2)^2,\]\[\omega_3\to (\omega_2+\omega_1,\omega_2+\omega)^2,\]\[\omega_2\to (\omega_1^{\omega+2}+2, \omega_1+2)^2.\]Establish whether the following is consistent with the generalised continuum hypothesis:\[\omega_2\to (\omega_1+\omega)_2^2,\]or even $\omega_2 \to (\xi)_2^2$ for all $\xi<\omega_2$.
